title: cyperus ephemerus
intro: Cyperus ephemerus, also known as the desert flatsedge or ephemeral flatsedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family and is native to Australia and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Cyperus ephemerus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs and colds, and promote digestive health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected area.
